page: Microsoft Azure D64pls_v5 is an ARM-based virtual machine belonging to the DPLSv5 server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 64 dedicated physical cores and 64 vCPUs powered by an Ampere Neoverse-N1 processor based on the ARMv8 architecture, operating at a clock speed of 3.0 GHz. The processor configuration includes 64 KB of L1D cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and 32 MB of L3 cache. The server is equipped with 128.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, though it is premium storage capable.

Benchmark data indicates strong multi-core and memory bandwidth capabilities. The server achieves a strong multi-core CPU performance score of 112418.18 in stress-ng tests, alongside a strong memory bandwidth read-only small block speed of 4383.1 GB/sec and large block speed of 177.02 GB/sec. In software compilation tests using Clang, the system registers a strong Geekbench score of 46168.0. It also demonstrates strong performance in Redis SET operations, processing 80.12 million operations per second, and strong text generation speeds of 26.75 tokens per second for medium-sized (7B) language models. Conversely, single-core performance and text processing benchmarks fall into average and weak categories respectively, highlighting a performance profile optimized for parallelized workloads rather than single-threaded execution.

Given its 1:2 RAM-to-vCPU ratio, the D64pls_v5 offers a cost-efficient profile for compute-intensive workloads that do not require massive memory allocations or local storage. The dedicated ARMv8 cores provide predictable performance scaling for multi-threaded applications. This server is suited for software compilation, containerized microservices, multi-threaded web serving, Redis caching layers, and medium-scale language model inference tasks.
description: The Microsoft Azure D64pls_v5 is an ARM-based server from the DPLSv5 family, utilizing 64 dedicated physical cores and 64 vCPUs on a 3.0 GHz Ampere Neoverse-N1 processor. It features 128.0 GB of system memory, providing 2.0 GB of RAM per core, with no local storage or GPUs. Operating under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this instance delivers strong multi-core performance, as evidenced by a stress-ng multi-core score of 112418.18 and high memory read bandwidth. While single-core and text processing benchmarks are average to weak, the server excels in parallelized tasks, showing strong results in software compilation, Redis operations, and medium-model LLM text generation. The 1:2 RAM-to-vCPU ratio makes it a cost-efficient choice for compute-intensive, multi-threaded workloads such as CI/CD pipelines, web serving, and distributed caching.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D64pls_v5 is an ARM-based server featuring 64 dedicated Ampere Neoverse-N1 cores, 128 GB of RAM, and strong multi-core performance optimized for parallel compute workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D64pls_v5 is a 64-vCPU ARM-based server with 128 GB RAM, delivering strong multi-core performance for compute-intensive workloads.
tagline: An ARM-based compute-focused server featuring sixty-four dedicated cores and high memory bandwidth for highly parallelized multi-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: 64 dedicated Ampere Neoverse-N1 ARMv8 cores running at 3.0 GHz
128.0 GB of system memory providing a 1:2 RAM-to-vCPU ratio
Strong multi-core performance and high memory read bandwidth
No local storage or integrated GPU resources included
Suited for software compilation, Redis caching, and multi-threaded web serving
